name: feat-ESE-0015-03
title: Camera dependency graph and topological sort
description: Build a dependency graph from cameras and render quads, then topologically sort to determine render order. A camera depends on another camera if it can see a RenderQuad displaying that camera's renderTarget. Detect cycles (hall of mirrors) and mark them for iterative resolution. Conservative visibility: all RenderQuads in the same world are assumed visible to any camera in that world.
acceptanceCriteria:
- buildDependencyGraph collects cameras and their renderTargets, finds RenderQuads referencing those targets
- topologicalSort returns cameras in render order (leaves first)
- Cycles detected and returned separately with recursionDepth
- Independent cameras (split screen) have no dependencies between them
- Linear dependencies (TV in scene) produce correct ordering: inner camera before outer
- Unit tested with mock camera/quad configurations
demoDeliverable: Terminal output from unit tests showing dependency graph construction and topological sort results.
testingScenarios:
- Two independent cameras (no quads): no dependencies, any order valid
- Camera A sees quad displaying camera B's target: B renders before A
- Three-camera chain (A sees B's quad, B sees C's quad): order is C, B, A
- Cycle: camera A sees quad displaying A's own target: detected as cycle with recursionDepth
- Mixed: some independent, some dependent cameras
stakeholderUnderstanding: Cameras that can see render quads impose a correct render order. This identifies the dependencies and sorts them.
